title: athroandra rivularis
intro: Athroandra rivularis, also known as the "Kampong" or "Wild Forest", is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Malaysia and Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Staphylococcaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Athroandra rivularis include treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as aiding in wound healing and reducing fever. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or topical creams, and is also used in traditional folk remedies to treat various ailments.
